name: Meal-planning apps
summary: Subscription meal-planning apps (Mealime, PlateJoy, Yummly Pro, Paprika one-time, etc.), $0-$60/year.

evidence.claim: USDA Dietary Guidelines for Americans 2020-2025 provide the federal nutrition recommendations against which app claims should be measured (macros, fiber, sodium).

Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics 2023 position on mobile nutrition apps: only 14% of reviewed apps cite credentialed source for nutrition claims; ~40% contain one or more inaccurate macro calculations.
FDA Food Labeling rules (21 CFR §101) define what nutrition claims like 'low-fat', 'high-fiber' can legally mean on packaged products; app-displayed data should match these thresholds.

criteria.name: dietitian_reviewed
criteria.notes: RD-credentialed nutrition review is rare in free apps (E2).

criteria.name: dietary_restrictions_supported
criteria.notes: Breadth of supported restrictions (gluten-free, vegan, keto, diabetic, allergen).

criteria.name: offline_mode
criteria.notes: Many 'always-online' apps lock users out when subscription lapses.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Often means one RD reviewed a sampling at launch, not all recipes (E2). Verify by checking whether per-recipe nutrition is RD-validated or crowd-sourced.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Does each recipe display an RD credential, or is the claim applied broadly to the catalog?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Most 'AI personalization' is rule-based filtering on cuisine/restriction tags. True personalization requires biometric input (glucose monitor, body composition), which most apps do not process.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Does the AI incorporate biometric or outcome data, or is it rule-based cuisine/ingredient filtering?

confabulationPatterns.reality: User-generated-recipe apps often show macros computed from ingredient estimates with 20-30% error (E2). FDA labeling thresholds are not enforced on non-packaged food (E3).
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the macro data sourced from USDA Foundation/Nutritionix/Edamam, or user-generated?

compatibilityQuestions: Dietary restrictions? Narrow restrictions (FODMAP, keto, renal diet) need specialized apps, not general ones.
Budget: do you want ad-free? Subscription apps remove ads; free apps are ad-heavy.

typicalHiddenCosts.name: Auto-renewal traps; many charge $50-$60/yr after free trial
Grocery delivery partner markup if integrated
